recommendation_visual_mappings: Rows represent participants, participant-days, bouts, or another defined observation unit; columns represent time, and colour encodes the metric value. | Each panel contains a time-series plot with time on the x-axis and the metric on the y-axis; panels separate participants, groups, days, or conditions.
recommendation_rationales: Shows many temporal profiles without overlapping a large number of lines. | Retains precise position encoding while separating overlapping series.
recommendation_use_when: Use for repeated days or participants when individual patterns matter. | Use when the number of participants, groups, or conditions is small.

recommendation_names: Paired dot plot or slope chart
recommendation_visual_mappings: The x-axis represents the two linked time points or conditions, the y-axis represents the metric value, and paired marks or lines connect the linked values. When only summaries are shown, points represent paired summary estimates and optional intervals represent uncertainty.
recommendation_rationales: Shows the direction and size of a two-level paired difference, either for observed units or for paired summary estimates.
recommendation_use_when: Use for two linked time points or conditions, including summary weekday-versus-weekend or pre-post estimates when individual paired values are not displayed.
recommendation_cautions: Do not draw participant-level connecting lines when only summary estimates are available; use a summary paired/dumbbell layout with clearly labelled intervals instead.

recommendation_cautions: Many trajectories can obscure the group pattern; consider a summary profile plus selected or transparent individual lines.
